a:r.a:t: Organic data-center → AVEVA PLM → API stack, with a simple step-by-step client HD Hyundai can run and verify.
A - Reference Architecture: organic Hardware <-> Software <-> API
-documented endpoints, token-secured; every call returns JSON + an HTTP status, so each step is verifiable.
2. AVEVA PLM SOFTWARE: application & engineering services
Versioned objects, structures/BOM & configuration rules, ECR/impact, approvals, all exposed through the API layer above.
PLM runs on ↔ data-center hosts, scales & secures it
B - HD Hyundai: Using the API (simple, step-by-step, verifiable)
plm_client.py - HD Hyundai PLM client -> calls REST API
Each step calls one endpoint and returns a status you can check (OK)
Organic connection: data-center hardware hosts AVEVA PLM Software → AVEVA PLM exposes secured APIs → HD Hyundai's client calls them in clear, status-verified Steps.
